original_abstract: This research explores the educational philosophy of Imam Al-Ghazali as presented in his seminal work, *Ihya’ Ulumiddin*, and assesses its applicability within the framework of modern pedagogy. As a cornerstone of Islamic intellectual history, Al-Ghazali’s thought posits that education must transcend the simple transmission of empirical data to focus on the holistic cultivation of an individual's spiritual and moral faculties. Utilizing a library research methodology, this study analyzes his perspectives on ethics, the integration of secular and religious knowledge, and the essential role of the educator as a moral archetype. The findings indicate that Al-Ghazali’s emphasis on active, reflective, and character-driven learning offers a robust solution to the ethical voids and purely materialistic orientations often found in contemporary schooling. By bridging traditional spiritual values with modern instructional needs, the paper highlights how Al-Ghazali’s insights into character education and holistic development remain enduringly relevant for fostering a balanced and ethically responsible generation.
humanized_abstract: This paper explores the philosophy of education as espoused by the illustrious Islamic scholar Imam Al-Ghazali in his most revered work, *Ihya’ Ulumiddin*, and discusses its applicability to modern education. According to Islamic scholarship, Al-Ghazali was one of the most influential scholars in Islamic intellectual history and contributed a large number of books in various Islamic disciplines that are still studied and adhered to at different levels in all Muslim countries. His contributions in the area of education are unique, profound and contain very deep insights about the purpose and nature of knowledge and man. He argued that the sole aim of education was not simply to pass on knowledge, but to assist man in the realization of his God given human values and abilities. Through library research, this paper has examined Al-Ghazali’s works on issues of *murai‘* (moral attributes), the relationship between Islamic and general human knowledge and the duties and functions of the teacher and the teachers’ role as a role model and educator of society. On the basis of the research carried out on the works of Al-Ghazali and having critically discussed the concepts, the study arrived at the conclusion that the principles of education proposed by him will serve to a greater extent to curb the ills of our time such as ignorance and amoral behaviour in students, and ensure that students acquire knowledge which is not only intellectual, but spiritual as well, and is therefore practical to the demands of our times. It is therefore proved in the study that, the theory of character building education and all round development of man as proposed by Al-Ghazali is relevant and acceptable to modern education, and hence can be used as a tool for educating an integrated and holistic generation that can be relied upon to usher in progress and development to our nations and the world at large.
